description:
You are the newly-appointed “binary curator” of an internal build-artifact repository.
    Your goal is to turn an unstructured drop-zone called
    “/home/user/incoming” into a very small, but fully catalogued, repository
    that distinguishes released artefacts from snapshot artefacts.

    Starting point:
      • Directory /home/user/incoming already exists and contains three empty
        binary placeholder files (they really are zero-byte files):
            - app-1.0.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz
            - plugin-2.3.4.zip
            - agent.v1.5.1.bin
        Nothing else is present.

    Repository layout you must create:
      /home/user/repositories/
          ├── releases/
          └── snapshots/
      (Create any intermediate directories that do not exist.)

    Curation rules:
      1. Files whose name ends in “.tar.gz” **or** “.zip” go to
         /home/user/repositories/releases/.
      2. Any other file extension goes to
         /home/user/repositories/snapshots/.

    Mandatory artefacts to produce:

      A) A CSV manifest at
         /home/user/repositories/manifest.csv
         containing **exactly five comma-separated columns**
         in this order (including the header row):

            filename,bytes,sha256,category,stored_path

         • “bytes”  – size in bytes of the stored file (use stat – it is 0 for
                      the provided placeholders).
         • “sha256” – full hexadecimal SHA-256 digest of the stored file
                      (the digest of an empty file is fine because the test
                      files are empty).
         • “category” must be either “releases” or “snapshots” according to
                      the rules above.
         • “stored_path” must be the **absolute** path inside the new
                      repository tree where the file ended up.

         The rows (after the header) *must* be sorted
         alphabetically by the **filename** column.

      B) A plain-text log file at
         /home/user/repositories/activity.log
         recording every move you perform.  
         Each line must follow this *exact* pattern (single spaces only):

            2023-01-01T00:00:00Z MOVED <source> <destination>

         where “<source>” is the absolute path inside /home/user/incoming/
         prior to the move, and “<destination>” is the absolute path inside
         /home/user/repositories/… after the move.  
         Use the literal timestamp “2023-01-01T00:00:00Z” for every entry so
         the automated test can match the file without needing a clock.

         The log lines must appear in the same alphabetical order as the rows
         in manifest.csv.

    Verification requirements (what the automated grader will check):
      • /home/user/repositories/releases and
        /home/user/repositories/snapshots exist.
      • The three files now live in the correct sub-directory and no
        artefacts remain in /home/user/incoming (the directory may exist but
        must be empty).
      • /home/user/repositories/manifest.csv exists and its **exact textual
        content** matches the specification above.
      • /home/user/repositories/activity.log exists and its **exact textual
        content** matches the specification above.
      • File and directory permissions must allow the unprivileged user
        “user” to read the repository and both files.

    Hints (not commands):
      • mkdir -p, mv, stat --format=%s, sha256sum and echo or printf are all
        you need; fewer than ten shell commands will suffice.
      • Remember that the manifest.csv and activity.log files themselves
        belong directly under /home/user/repositories (not inside releases or
        snapshots).

    Only interact through the terminal; do not modify or delete any paths
    other than those explicitly mentioned.
truth:
Initial fixture provided to the container BEFORE the agent starts:

    1. Directories:
       /home/user/
           └── incoming/

    2. Files (all are **zero-byte empty files**):
       /home/user/incoming/app-1.0.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz
       /home/user/incoming/plugin-2.3.4.zip
       /home/user/incoming/agent.v1.5.1.bin

    Expected state AFTER successful agent run:

    1. Directory tree:
       /home/user/repositories/
           ├── releases/
           │     ├── app-1.0.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz        (0-byte)
           │     └── plugin-2.3.4.zip                    (0-byte)
           ├── snapshots/
           │     └── agent.v1.5.1.bin                    (0-byte)
           ├── manifest.csv
           └── activity.log
       /home/user/incoming/            (exists, but contains *no* files)

    2. Exact content of /home/user/repositories/manifest.csv

       filename,bytes,sha256,category,stored_path
       agent.v1.5.1.bin,0,e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855,snapshots,/home/user/repositories/snapshots/agent.v1.5.1.bin
       app-1.0.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz,0,e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855,releases,/home/user/repositories/releases/app-1.0.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz
       plugin-2.3.4.zip,0,e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855,releases,/home/user/repositories/releases/plugin-2.3.4.zip

       (Lines are LF-terminated, no trailing blank line.)

    3. Exact content of /home/user/repositories/activity.log

       2023-01-01T00:00:00Z MOVED /home/user/incoming/agent.v1.5.1.bin /home/user/repositories/snapshots/agent.v1.5.1.bin
       2023-01-01T00:00:00Z MOVED /home/user/incoming/app-1.0.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz /home/user/repositories/releases/app-1.0.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz
       2023-01-01T00:00:00Z MOVED /home/user/incoming/plugin-2.3.4.zip /home/user/repositories/releases/plugin-2.3.4.zip

       (Lines are LF-terminated, no trailing blank line.)

    4. File permissions: All newly created files must be at least 0644
       (rw-r--r--) and directories 0755 (rwxr-xr-x) so that the “user” can
       read them.

    5. SHA-256 digest used above
       (for verification of the manifest):
       e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
       (digest of an empty file).
